assignee.description: Static assignee/queue label; the fallback when no `assignmentDecision` routes the Task (┬¦5.3).

assignmentDecision.description: DecisionModel that routes the assignee/queue ŌĆö "assignment routing is itself a Decision" (┬¦5.3).

assignIn.description: Blackboard variables passed, in order, to the assignment Decision.

slaMs.description: Pausable SLA budget in milliseconds (┬¦5.7); paused intervals are excluded from this accounting.

out.description: Blackboard variable the resolution (or escalation `onTimeout`) value is written to.

onTimeout.description: Auto-decide fallback value written to `out` when the SLA budget is exhausted (the escalation path, ┬¦5.2).

escalationQueue.description: Supervisor queue the Task escalates to on SLA expiry; defaults to `supervisor` (┬¦5.2 escalation chain).

description:
A `human-task` step ŌĆö the first-party Case/Task module's manual-review primitive (doc 04 ┬¦5.2): a
`createTask` activity, then an **await-signal raced by a pausable SLA timer**. Phase 3.3 adds the Case
layer around it:
- **assignment-as-Decision** (┬¦5.3): when `assignmentDecision` is set, the assignee/queue is *routed*
by evaluating that DecisionModel over `assignIn` (governed like any Decision) rather than a static
`assignee` label.
- **pausable SLA** (┬¦5.7): a `pause` signal (task enters `awaiting-applicant`) stops the SLA clock and
a `resume` signal restarts it; the paused interval is excluded from SLA accounting and recorded as
distinct clock-stop events. Time-skip makes a month-long budget verify in milliseconds.
- **escalation** (┬¦5.2): if the budget is exhausted before resolution, the task **escalates** to
`escalationQueue` (supervisor) and `onTimeout` is written to `out` as the auto-decide fallback.
